skill.name: request-refactor-plan
skill.description: Create a detailed refactor plan with tiny commits via user interview, then file it as a GitHub issue. Use when user wants to plan a refactor, create a refactoring RFC, or break a refactor into safe incremental steps.
skill.summary: Create a detailed refactor plan with tiny commits via user interview, then file it as a GitHub issue...
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged five weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but each location is ordinary planning prose in SKILL.md. No evidence found of cryptographic code, executable scripts, network exfiltration, environment access, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detections
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain prose about creating a detailed refactor plan, asking for a description, creating a GitHub issue, testing behavior, and out-of-scope notes. No weak cryptographic algorithm or crypto implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is direct: every flagged location is natural-language refactoring guidance. The skill has no code, imports, commands, crypto APIs, encoded payloads, or executable artifacts.

content.value_statement: Large refactors often fail because scope, tradeoffs, and testing plans are unclear. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a structured interview and produces a GitHub issue plan.
actual_capabilities: Interviews the user about the refactor goal, constraints, and solution ideas.
Directs the assistant to inspect the repository before accepting assumptions.
Prompts discussion of alternative approaches and implementation boundaries.
Breaks refactor work into tiny commits that should keep the codebase working.
Captures decisions, testing plans, out-of-scope items, and further notes.
Formats the result as a GitHub issue description for team review.
limitations: It creates a plan, not the refactor implementation itself.
GitHub issue creation depends on the assistant environment and available permissions.
Plan quality depends on repository access and the user's answers.
It does not guarantee that every proposed commit is risk free.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Framework Migration
use_cases.description: Create a staged plan for migrating code while keeping tests and behavior stable.

use_cases.title: Turn Refactor Ideas into an RFC
use_cases.description: Convert rough refactor goals into a decision document with scope and testing notes.

use_cases.title: Review Risk Before Starting Work
use_cases.description: Use repository inspection and user interview questions to expose missing assumptions early.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Refactor
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a structured plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use request-refactor-plan to help me plan a refactor. Interview me first, inspect the repository, and create a GitHub issue plan.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Incremental Commit Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on safe sequencing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use request-refactor-plan for this refactor idea. Break the work into very small commits and include testing decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Refactor Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use request-refactor-plan to evaluate my proposed approach, present alternatives, and document the final decision in an issue plan.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Team RFC
prompt_templates.prompt: Use request-refactor-plan to build a review-ready RFC. Include problem statement, solution, tiny commits, decisions, testing, and out-of-scope work.

output_examples.input: I want to refactor our authentication flow, but I am not sure where to start.
output_examples.output: A structured interview, repository findings, alternative approaches, and a GitHub issue plan with small commits and testing decisions.

output_examples.input: We need to split a large module without breaking product behavior.
output_examples.output: A refactor plan that defines scope, module boundaries, commit sequence, behavioral tests, and out-of-scope cleanup.

output_examples.input: Help me prepare a proposal for a risky codebase migration.
output_examples.output: A decision document that records tradeoffs, implementation boundaries, test coverage needs, and review-ready migration steps.

best_practices: Provide the business problem, known constraints, and any solution ideas before planning.
Let the assistant inspect relevant code and tests before finalizing the plan.
Review the generated issue with the team before implementation begins.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to skip repository investigation before planning.
Do not treat the generated plan as a substitute for code review.
Do not include file paths or code snippets in the final issue if they may become outdated.

faq.question: Does this skill change code?
faq.answer: No. It guides planning and creates a refactor issue description.

faq.question: Can it create the GitHub issue automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, when the assistant environment has GitHub access and permission to create issues.

faq.question: Does it inspect the repository?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the assistant to verify assumptions by exploring the repository.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Provide the problem, desired outcome, constraints, and any ideas you already considered.

faq.question: Is it useful for small refactors?
faq.answer: Yes, but it is most useful when scope, risk, or sequencing needs discussion.

faq.question: Does it include testing guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks about test coverage and records testing decisions in the issue plan.
